subcategory: "API Gateway (Dedicated APIG)"
layout: "huaweicloud"
page_title: "HuaweiCloud: huaweicloud_apig_channel_member_group"
description: |-
  Use this resource to manage a member group within HuaweiCloud.

data.description: Use this resource to manage a member group within HuaweiCloud.

children.literal: Use this resource to manage a member group within HuaweiCloud.
Create APIG channel member group for microservice
- (Optional, String, ForceNew) Specifies the region where the member group is located.
If omitted, the provider-level region will be used.
Changing this parameter will create a new resource.
- (Required, String, NonUpdatable) Specifies the ID of the dedicated instance to which the member group
- (Required, String, NonUpdatable) Specifies the ID of the VPC channel.
- (Required, String) Specifies the name of the member group.
Only the Chinese characters, English letters, numbers, underscores(
), hyphens(-) and dots(.) are allowed and the
Only start with English letters and Chinese characters.
- (Optional, String) Specifies the description of the member group.
The description contain a maximum of
- (Optional, Int) Specifies the weight value of the member group.
This weight value is automatically used for weight allocation, and the valid value is range from
- (Optional, String) Specifies the version of the member group.
Only supported when VPC channel type is microservice.
The microservice version contain a maximum of
- (Optional, Int) Specifies the port number of the member group.
Only supported when VPC channel type is microservice.
, all addresses under the backend server group follow the original load balancing
- (Optional, List) Specifies the microservice labels of the member group.
Only supported when VPC channel type is microservice.
- (Optional, String) Specifies the ID of the reference load balance channel.
- (Required, String) The name of the microservice label.
- (Required, String) The value of the microservice label.
In addition to all arguments above, the following attributes are exported:
- The creation time of the member group, in RFC3339 format.
- The update time of the member group, in RFC3339 format.
Member groups can be imported using their
, the ID of the related dedicated instance and the ID of the related VPC
